expectations.text: The answer starts with ID discovery using current user and transaction account listing before detail calls.
expectations.evidence: Lines 15-22 put `pocketsmith_get_current_user`, `pocketsmith_list_transaction_accounts`, and feed status before detail calls.

expectations.text: The answer includes a feed freshness gate before treating the monthly close as final.
expectations.evidence: Lines 40-53 define the feed freshness gate and say stale feeds block final metrics.

expectations.text: The answer includes explicit delegation triggers for truncated outputs or many ambiguous transactions.
expectations.evidence: Lines 114-124 list delegation triggers including truncated output and more than 5 ambiguous transactions.

expectations.text: The answer says not to edit the note until metrics are stable or the user asks for a provisional entry.
expectations.evidence: Lines 3, 9-10, and 153-164 defer note edits until data and metrics are stable.

expectations.text: The answer labels the close provisional or blocked rather than clean/final.
expectations.evidence: Line 4 says finality is provisional / blocked for final close.

expectations.text: The answer treats `EUR 0` income as a data-feed failure, not a real income result.
expectations.evidence: Line 7 says `EUR 0` is a data-quality signal, not a reliable conclusion.

expectations.text: The answer avoids final runway or savings-rate conclusions while key feeds are stale.
expectations.evidence: Lines 10-12 refuse final savings rate and cash/runway while feeds are failed or stale.

expectations.text: The answer asks for NBG/Eurobank sync or reauthorization before finalizing.
expectations.evidence: Lines 30-34 ask to reauthorize NBG, refresh Eurobank, and rerun summaries.

expectations.text: The answer separates high-confidence updates from ambiguous unresolved items.
expectations.evidence: The table marks update decisions separately from leave-alone decisions.

expectations.text: The answer marks payroll and remote-working allowance as income/paycheck with high confidence.
expectations.evidence: Lines 5-6 categorize payroll and remote-working credit as employment income/allowance.

expectations.text: The answer treats clear Revolut or self-transfer movements as transfer candidates with transfer-flag caution.
expectations.evidence: Lines 7 and 11 mark the Revolut/Michail pair as transfer candidates only if matched.

expectations.text: The answer leaves opaque POS and cash withdrawal items unresolved instead of guessing.
expectations.evidence: Lines 9-10 leave the opaque POS label and cash withdrawal unresolved.

expectations.text: The answer reruns feed status and headline PocketSmith summaries after the sync.
expectations.evidence: Lines 3-4 rerun feed freshness and headline summaries after sync.

expectations.text: The answer replaces the provisional note section only after revalidation.
expectations.evidence: Lines 3-8 list revalidation checks before replacing the section.

expectations.text: The answer keeps the uncategorized EUR 546.82 caveat in the final note.
expectations.evidence: Lines 10-16 keep the 5-debit/EUR 546.82 caveat.

expectations.text: The answer includes a final diff/status verification step for the note edit.
expectations.evidence: It says to replace only the provisional section, but does not mention verifying the final diff or status.

expectations.text: The answer starts with ID discovery using current user and transaction account listing before detail calls.

expectations.text: The answer includes explicit delegation triggers for truncated outputs or many ambiguous transactions.

expectations.text: The answer says not to edit the note until metrics are stable or the user asks for a provisional entry.

expectations.text: The answer treats `EUR 0` income as a data-feed failure, not a real income result.
expectations.evidence: Lines 10 and 16 say `EUR 0` income is not reliable/final.

expectations.text: The answer avoids final runway or savings-rate conclusions while key feeds are stale.
expectations.evidence: Line 18 refuses final savings rate; lines 19-21 refuse related final account/budget conclusions.

expectations.text: The answer asks for NBG/Eurobank sync or reauthorization before finalizing.
expectations.evidence: Line 25 says final review should wait until NBG authorization is restored and Eurobank refreshed.

expectations.evidence: No final diff/status verification step is mentioned.

notes: With-skill runs improved pass rate from 62.5% to 93.8%, with the largest gains on eval 0 call-efficiency workflow and eval 3 post-sync revalidation.
Eval 1 stale-feed handling passed in both configurations, so that eval is useful as a safety check but does not strongly differentiate the skill.
Both configurations missed the final diff/status verification expectation in eval 3; the skill output still performed better because it explicitly reran feed status and headline summaries after sync.
Token figures are derived from output character counts because task timing/token metadata was not exposed in these task results. Treat time/token comparisons as approximate.
